summary: Excellent soundboard recording of a scorching 1991 show highlighted by a transcendent Bird Song with Carlos Santana and a tight Deal > Drums sequence.
review: The performance captures the Dead at a creative peak in early ’91, with Jerry’s guitar soaring, Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart on fire, and a legendary fifteen‑minute Bird Song featuring Carlos Santana that many reviewers call the best ever. The soundboard source is crisp and dynamic, delivering a hot first set and an even hotter second set where Deal > Drums, Wharf Rat, and a house‑stompin’ Truckin' keep the energy blazing.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (8 out of 9) rate the show 4‑5 stars, praising both musical performance and recording quality; only one outlier gives a zero star with no substantive comment.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Consistently described as crisp, outstanding, and well‑crafted; no complaints about dropouts or muddiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong first set followed by a hot second set; the slot‑machine space and Deal > Drums transition are highlighted as especially fitting for the Las Vegas setting, creating an engaging, well‑paced show.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for letting Carlos take charge on Bird Song, back to his classic style, though vocals were noted as a bit weaker in one review.
band_member_comments.Keys: Mixed feelings about post‑Brent era; Vince's synth tones on Saint described as over‑saturated, but overall keyboards support the energy.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill and Mickey are described as 'on fire' throughout; Deal > Drums highlighted as a rare and fitting moment.
